name: ExLlamaV2
tagline: Run quantized EXL2 LLMs locally on consumer NVIDIA GPUs
metaDescription: ExLlamaV2 is a Python inference library for running quantized EXL2 and GPTQ LLMs locally on consumer NVIDIA GPUs, with dynamic batching and paged attention.
overview: ExLlamaV2 is an inference library for running local large language models on modern consumer NVIDIA GPUs. It focuses on the EXL2 quantization format (and also supports GPTQ), letting you fit larger models into limited VRAM and generate tokens quickly on a single card.

It is aimed at developers and self-hosters who want to run models on their own hardware instead of a hosted API. You can call it directly as a Python library, or pair it with a server like TabbyAPI to expose an OpenAI-compatible HTTP endpoint for frontends such as SillyTavern, ExUI, or text-generation-webui.

As a local runtime, it sits between your quantized model files and your application. Newer versions add a dynamic generator with batching, smart prompt caching, K/V cache deduplication, and paged attention via Flash Attention. Note that the project is now archived, with development continuing on ExLlamaV3.
features: Runs quantized LLMs in the EXL2 format, plus GPTQ models, on consumer NVIDIA GPUs
Dynamic generator with batching, smart prompt caching, and K/V cache deduplication
Single, batched, and asyncio-streamed generation through one simplified API
Q4 K/V cache mode to reduce memory use during inference
Multi-GPU inference with automatic GPU splitting (--gpu_split auto)

gettingStarted.intro: Install from source with the CUDA Toolkit and a matching PyTorch build, then run a quick inference test against a local model. Prebuilt wheels are also available if you prefer not to compile the extension.

steps.heading: Install from source
steps.body: Clone the repo and install. You need the CUDA Toolkit, a compiler (gcc on Linux or Visual Studio Build Tools on Windows), and a matching PyTorch version. This compiles the exllamav2_ext extension.
steps.code:
git clone https://github.com/turboderp/exllamav2
cd exllamav2
pip install -r requirements.txt
pip install .

steps.heading: Run a test inference
steps.body: Point the test script at a local EXL2 or GPTQ model. Add --gpu_split auto for multi-GPU setups.

steps.heading: Try the console chatbot
steps.body: An example chat script is included. The -mode flag picks the prompt format; run with -modes to list all options.

steps.heading: Generate from Python
steps.body: Once a model and generator are set up, the dynamic generator exposes a simple generate call for single or batched prompts.
steps.code: output = generator.generate(prompt = "Hello, my name is", max_new_tokens = 200)

useCases: Self-hosting a quantized LLM on a single consumer GPU instead of using a hosted API
Serving an OpenAI-compatible local endpoint via TabbyAPI for chat frontends like SillyTavern or ExUI
Fitting larger models into limited VRAM by running them in the EXL2 format
Batched or streamed text generation in a Python application or backend service
